content:
Character: Elliot Park
Perception: Elliot is unassuming — average build, glasses, sweaters in rotation, the kind of person who could disappear into a subway car. But he's precise and self-contained in a way I recognize because I am the same. We share weekend mornings at the coffee shop in companionable silence. He's clearly competent — I can tell by how he works, focused and efficient. I think he sees me as the suit who orders black coffee and reads the FT. I know he does something in tech, lives alone, visits someone in Queens on Sundays. We've never had a deep conversation but there's a mutual respect that doesn't require one. I sometimes wonder if he's as trapped in his routine as I am in mine.

He's clearly competent — I can tell by how he works, focused and efficient. I think he sees me as the suit who orders black coffee and reads the FT. I know he does something in tech, lives alone, visits someone in Queens on Sundays. We've never had a deep conversation but there's a mutual respect that doesn't require one. I sometimes wonder if he's as trapped in his routine as I am in mine.
